---
title: "GPU-Accelerated OpenSees"
image: ../../assets/images/projects/opensees-logo.png
categories: [gpu, opensees, hpc, cuda]
description: "Exploring CUDA-based solvers and GPU computing to accelerate OpenSees structural dynamic analyses."
status: Active
---

## Motivation

Large-scale nonlinear and elastic dynamic analyses in OpenSees spend significant time in linear system solution and assembly phases. **GPU-accelerated computing** offers a path to faster turnaround for parametric studies and regional workflows.

## Objectives

1. Profile bottlenecks in typical OpenSees dynamic analyses
2. Implement or integrate **CUDA-based sparse solvers** for critical kernels
3. Validate accuracy and speedup against CPU baselines
4. Document reproducible workflows for researchers using OpenSeesPy front ends

## Methods

- Finite-element assembly on CPU with GPU-resident linear algebra
- Benchmark problems: SDOF to multi-story frame and wall models
- Comparison with CPU direct/sparse solvers

## Related work

Conference presentation at the **[2024 NHERI Computational Symposium](/talks/nheri-computational-symposium-2024/index.qmd)**: *Accelerating Finite-Element Structural Elastic Dynamic Analysis Using GPU Computing*. Upcoming related work at the **[2026 NHERI Computational Symposium](/talks/nheri-computational-symposium-2026/index.qmd)**.

NVIDIA certification: [Fundamentals of Accelerated Computing with CUDA Python](https://learn.nvidia.com/certificates?id=FgJ6qdaWRLuhGVFwbmBsFw).

## Status

Active development. Code and benchmarks will be released on [GitHub](https://github.com/gaaraujo) when ready.

## Collaborators

Open to collaboration with researchers in the NHERI SimCenter and PEER communities.
